chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْعُمْرَةِ الْمَبْتُولَةِ فِي أَشْهُرِ الْحَجِّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 207 – The exclusive Umra during the months of Hajj

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/234)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/234)
text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ خَرَجَ فِي أَشْهُرِ الْحَجِّ مُعْتَمِراً ثُمَّ رَجَعَ إِلَى بِلَادِهِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ وَ إِنْ حَجَّ فِي عَامِهِ ذَلِكَ وَ أَفْرَدَ الْحَجَّ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَمٌ فَإِنَّ الْحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) خَرَجَ قَبْلَ التَّرْوِيَةِ بِيَوْمٍ إِلَى الْعِرَاقِ وَ قَدْ كَانَ دَخَلَ مُعْتَمِراً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Ibrahim Bin Umar Al Yamani,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having been asked about a man who went out during the months of Hajj to perform Umra, then he returned to his city. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem; and even if performed Hajj during that year of his, and exclusive Hajj, so there is no blood (of a sacrificial animal) upon him, fro Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> went out to Al-Iraq before Al-Tarawiyya by a day, and he<sup>asws</sup> had entered to perform Umra’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and Muhammad ibn ‘Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Ibrahim ibn ‘Umar al-Yemaniy who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about a man who travels in the months of al-Hajj for al-‘Umrah, then returns to his town. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful. If he performs al-Hajj in that year he is not required to offer animal offering. Al-Husayn ibn Ali, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, left before the day of al-Tarwiyah (eight of Dhu al-Hajj ah) for Iraq and he had entered Makkah for al-‘Umrah.’” NOTE: (Fatwa best explains Hadith 2-3 of this Chapter.)
